title: Diostea scoparia
intro: Diostea scoparia, also known as Yerba Buena, is a plant species belonging to the family Verbenaceae, native to South America, particularly in the Andes mountains. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and alleviate respiratory problems. Bioactive constituents of Diostea scoparia include flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Diostea scoparia is commonly used in traditional herbal medicine and is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction to treat a range of ailments.
